titleStmt.title: Copper plates from Kondavidu (set III) ‚ÄĒ reign of PrŐ•thivńęŇõrńęmŇęla, year 43

titleStmt.title: Copper plates from Kondavidu (set III) ‚ÄĒ reign of PrŐ•thivńęŇõrńęmŇęla, year 43

editionStmt.p: First digital edition made by √Čcole fran√ßaise d'Extr√™me-Orient (Paris, France), realized in collaboration with the HiSoMA Research Centre (Lyon, France) and hosted by TGIR Huma-Num (France) as Early Inscriptions of ńÄndhradeŇõa, in 2015-2017.

projectDesc.p: The project DHARMA has received funding from the European Research Council (ERC) under the European Union's Horizon 2020 research and innovation programme (grant agreement no 809994).

prefixDef.p: Public URIs with the prefix bib to point to a Zotero Group Library named ERC-DHARMA whose data are open to the public.
Internal URIs using the part prefix to point to person elements in the DHARMA_IdListMembers_v01.xml file.

revisionDesc.change: EIAD file transformed to follow the DHARMA encoding structure. Metadata extracted to be checked and updated according DHARMA workflow. Done through XSLT.

div.ab:
svasti *ŇõrńęvijayanandivarddhanavńĀsakńĀd devadvijagurucaraŠĻáńĀravinda
samńĀrńĀdhanńĀdhigatavipulaniratiŇõayapuŠĻáyanicayasya sakala
diŠĻÖmaŠĻáŠłćalńĀlaŠĻĀkńĀrabhŇętayaŇõasaŠł• ŇõrńęprabhńĀkaravikhyńĀtamahńĀrńĀja
sya sŇęnuŠł• mńĀtńĀpitrŐ•pńĀdńĀnuddhyńĀtaŠł• ŇõrutismrŐ•tivihitapadńĀrtthńĀvabodha
janitapraj√ĪńĀvivekaniravagńętńĀnupńĀlitńĀŇõeŠĻ£avarŠĻáŠĻáńĀŇõramadha
rmmaŠł• prajńĀparirakŠĻ£aŠĻáopajanitapuŠĻáyopacayaprayojanńĀvńĀptarńĀ
jyagurubhńĀraŠł• pŇęrvvarńĀjarŠĻ£imaryyńĀdńĀnatikramaŠĻáńęyadharmmaŠł• AsakrŐ•
danekaniŇõitanistriŠĻĀŇõasahasrasaŠĻĀkulńĀtibhńęmasaŠĻĀgrńĀmabhŇęmiprati
2labdhavijayńę ŇõaŇõńęva prajńĀnandakaraŠł• udadhir iva gabhńęrasvatvaŠł•
Aharahas trisandhyńĀm iŠĻ£ŠĻ≠adevatńĀrńĀdhanena gobhŇęmihiraŠĻáyakanyńĀ
pradńĀnńĀdibhiŇõ ca saphalńękrŐ•takńĀyacittopakaraŠĻáaŠł• paramamńĀ
heŇõvaraŠł• paramabrahmaŠĻáyaŠł• Ňõrńęman¬∑ prithiviŇõrńęmŇęlarńĀjaŠł• svaviŠĻ£ayani
vńĀsinaŠł• sarvvńĀn evam ńĀj√ĪńĀpayati yathńĀ viditam astu bhavatńĀm¬∑ bhuvanavi
dite ŇõrńęguŠĻáapńĀŇõapure ŇõailendrapŇęrvvadigbhńĀganitambe matpratiŠĻ£ŠĻ≠hńĀpita
ŇõrńęlokavikhyńĀtamahńĀvihńĀranivńĀsinaŠł• AŠĻ£ŠĻ≠ńĀdaŇõanikńĀyacńĀtu
rddiŇõńĀryyavarabhikŠĻ£usaŠĻĀghasya cńęvarapiŠĻáŠłćapńĀtaŇõaiyyńĀsanaglńĀnapra
3pratyayabhaiŠĻ£ajyapariŠĻ£karopabhogńĀya tńĀŠłĽupńĀkkaviŠĻ£aye karakuŠĻáŠłćakaŠĻĀ
sńĀryyaveŠł∑kaŠĻáŠłćakurikimaŠĻáimarddipallave√ĪcanńĀmadheyagrńĀmamaddhyagataŠł• AparaŇõailanivńĀsinan navakarmmika
m adhiŠĻ£ŠĻ≠hńĀya ArŠĻ≠ŠĻ≠aluvuŠł∑¬∑ nńĀma grńĀmas sarvvabńĀdhaparihńĀreŠĻáa mayńĀ dattaŠł• tatra na ke
na cit paribńĀdhńĀ karaŠĻáńęyńĀ bhavatti cńĀtra manugńętńĀ ŇõlokńĀŠł•

pravarddhamńĀnavijayarńĀjyasaŠĻĀvatsare
tracatvńĀriŠĻĀŇõati varttamńĀne phńĀlguŠĻáamńĀsaŇõuklapakŠĻ£asya
trŐ•tiyńĀyńĀm¬∑ varttamńĀnńĀyńĀŠĻĀ ŇõńĀsanam aŠĻĀkitam¬∑ .

app.note: The first word is engraved in the left margin.
There is no kra to correct to krŐ• . See a clear example of krŐ• in l. 10.
Krishna Sastry does not note the scribal deletion.
A kńĀkapńĀda is marked above the Ňõa, but no correction or insertion seems to be made nor is any expected.
According to Krishna Sastry, this must be corrected to -bńĀdhńĀpari-. But the reading -bńĀdhńĀpari- is not attested anywhere in these inscriptions.
Krishna Sastry emends to bhatrŐ•-. Neither Krishna Sastry‚Äô reading nor his emendation is correct.
Despite the reading, the interpretation of Krishna Sastry and his contemporaries must have been that this must be emended to tricatvńĀriŠĻĀŇõati, and this is indeed what must be done.

div.p: Hail! From the victorious camp of Nandivardhana, the illustrious king PrŐ•thivńęŇõrńęmŇęla, blessed by the feet of his parents; upholder of all laws on class and stage of life, in impeccable manner, through [his] intelligence and discrimination produced by [his] perception of the meanings of the words enjoined in revelation and tradition; who had attained the heavy burden of sovereignty with the purpose of accumulating merit produced by protecting his subjects; for whom the rules of the Old Royal Sage (i.e. Manu) were an unbreakable law; who had won victory more than once on battle grounds that were excessively terrifying, dense with thousands of swords sharpened many times; who, like the moon, brought pleasure to his subjects; who, like the ocean, was of profound nature; who had brought fruit to his bodily and mental activities by daily worship of his preferred deity at the three junctures and by grants of cows, lands, gold and maidens, etc.; devout worshiper of MaheŇõvara; extremely devout; son of the celebrated great king Ňörńę PrabhńĀkara, whose fame was the ornament of all quarters of space, whose unsurpassed store of merit had been obtained by the veneration of the feet of gods, brahmins and masters ‚ÄĒ he commands all residents of his (?) own district, as follows:

‚ÄėBe it known to you that, having endorsed (adhiŠĻ£ŠĻ≠hńĀya) the officer in charge of constructions and renovations residing on the AparaŇõaila (to oversee the gift), the village named ArŠĻ≠ŠĻ≠aluvuŠł∑, being in the middle of the villages called KarakuŠĻáŠłća, KaŠĻĀsńĀryya, VeŠł∑kaŠĻáŠłća, Kuriki, MaŠĻáimarddi and Pallave√Īca, in the TńĀŠłĽupńĀka district, has been given by me‚ÄĒwith complete exemption of taxes‚ÄĒfor the purpose of the requisites consisting in robe, alms-food, lodging, as well as medicine to cure the sick, of the community of noble excellent monks of the four quarters and eighteen schools, residing in the great monastery called Ňörńę-Loka, that had been established by myself on the eastern slope of the eminent mountain at the world-renowned Ňörńę-GuŠĻáapńĀŇõapura. No one may make any trouble in this connection. And there are verses pronounced by Manu on this matter:
The destiny of those who slay their master, a cow, a brahmin, a child, a woman or an ascetic, will be the one of him who seizes [the land/livelihood] that is marked by a (royal) charter.
Hear the fruit of the virtuous man who protects it scrupulously: during numerous thousands of aeons he plays in heaven with the gods.
There is not and will not be any higher gift than the gift of land. There is not and will not be any worse sin than the theft of the same.
The giver of land stays in heaven for sixty thousand years; the one who seizes [land] as well as the one who approves (of the seizing) will reside as many [years] in hell.
He who would seize land given by himself or by someone else becomes a worm in excrement and is cooked together with his ancestors.
By numerous [kings], land has been given; and by many it has been protected. Whoever holds land at a given moment, to him does the fruit then belong.
The charter has been dated in the forty-third year, current, of the victorious and prosperous reign, on the third [day], current, of the waxing fortnight of the month of PhńĀlguŠĻáa.
As long as moon, sun and oceans [last], and as long as this earth [lasts], so long may the command of Ňörńę MŇęlarńĀja protect this land!
First described and edited by : 74-5, 80-2. Re-edited here from the estampages published by Krishna Sastry and after autopsy of the plates.
